verses.text: A man to whom God gives wealth and honour yet not the power to enjoy it, while a stranger consumes it, suffers a sore evil and vanity. Riches without enjoyment are useless. The ability to enjoy God's gifts is itself a gift He alone gives.

A man to whom God gives wealth and honour yet not the power to enjoy it, while a stranger consumes it, suffers a sore evil and vanity. Riches without enjoyment are useless. The ability to enjoy God's gifts is itself a gift He alone gives.

Though a man have many children and long life, if his soul is not filled with good and he has no burial, 'an untimely birth is better than he.' Length and abundance without contentment are empty. A long life unblessed is worse than none at all.

Though a man have many children and long life, if his soul is not filled with good and he has no burial, 'an untimely birth is better than he.' Length and abundance without contentment are empty. A long life unblessed is worse than none at all.

All a man's labour is for his mouth, yet the appetite is never filled; 'better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the desire.' Craving outruns satisfaction. Contentment with what is at hand beats endless restless longing.

All a man's labour is for his mouth, yet the appetite is never filled; 'better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the desire.' Craving outruns satisfaction. Contentment with what is at hand beats endless restless longing.

What man is has long been named, and he cannot contend with One mightier; who knows what is good in this fleeting, shadow-like life? Man is small before God. We cannot see ahead or argue with the One who made us.
